what_it_does: Renders a fixed-size HTML visual showing a workflow icon strip converging into a central hub and branching out to six role nodes, with title text, blueprint texture, footer hatch, and a small V mark.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans, an external vars stylesheet, and CSS custom properties for type, spacing, and color.
The layout is a centered 1600x900 static composition with absolute-positioned title, diagram, background plan texture, mark, and footer hatch.
The diagram is an inline SVG containing seven thin-line workflow icons on a dashed rail, a hub node, dashed routing lines, arrow markers, and six person-role nodes.
Role A through Role E share warm/gold node styling, while Role F uses a subtler bronze/base mix and secondary text.
There is no JavaScript and no interactive behavior.
standouts: The visual metaphor is routing: multiple tools converge into a hub and then fan out to role-specific endpoints.
The icon strip spans several workflow categories: house, checklist, floorplan, chat, gear, calendar, and chart.
The file uses linework, dashed connectors, and muted construction-plan styling to frame the diagram.
conventions: Keeps HTML, CSS, and SVG in a single file.
Uses CSS variables extensively instead of literal palette values for most styling.
Uses absolute positioning and a fixed artboard rather than responsive document flow.
Uses semantic-neutral placeholder role labels rather than named personas.
concerns: The 1600x900 fixed view may not adapt to smaller or larger screens without external scaling.
The SVG defs include an empty g with id ic that is not used.
The file depends on external font and stylesheet resources for its intended rendering.
notes: Static presentation file for a role-routing workflow diagram centered on a hub-and-branches concept.
questions: What real role names should replace Role A through Role F?
Is Role F intentionally visually differentiated from the other roles?
Are the seven top icons meant to correspond to specific product tools or workflow stages?
purpose_vs_actual: The title states an icon strip plus routing tree, and the file renders exactly that as a static visual diagram.
intention_signals: It appears intended to communicate that different workflow tools feed into a shared hub which distributes the appropriate view or output to each role.
novelty: Combines a top workflow icon rail with a central hub and lower role tree in one static SVG composition.
contribution: Contributes a neutral hub-and-role routing visual pattern, including the icon-strip-to-hub-to-role flow, dashed connector language, and role endpoint treatment for a fused system to retain as a substrate.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS/SVG artboard depicting seven workflow icons feeding a central hub that branches to six role nodes in a muted construction-diagram style.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for expressing one workflow routed through a hub into role-specific views using a clean visual diagram.
